projectContext: F1Predict is a portfolio-grade Formula 1 race prediction / strategy / scenario web app with a dark 'Pit Wall' broadcast aesthetic (motorsport timing-screen look: monospaced numbers, team colours, dense data tables and charts). Audience is technical recruiters and engineers evaluating it as a portfolio piece, so the bar is data integrity and polish: numbers must make sense (no NaN, no impossible probabilities, win% sensible), panels must be populated, layouts clean and uncluttered at desktop width. Calibrate harshly for anything that would make a recruiter doubt the work; the look is intentionally dense/technical, not minimalist consumer.

ratingScale.5: Embarrassing — fix immediately, makes the app look broken (NaN, empty panel, broken layout, impossible numbers)
ratingScale.4: Trust-eroding — degrades perceived polish; a recruiter would notice and lower their estimate
ratingScale.3: Worth improving — affects flow or readability but isn't a dealbreaker
ratingScale.2: Minor polish — would feel a touch nicer if fixed, low priority
ratingScale.1: Nit — pure preference, almost no real-world impact
